messages.content:
Savey said on 01 December 2023

It appears the BBC One Christmas idents this year are focusing on CBBC show KikoRiki, Good considering the characters in that show are anthropomorphic animals, making them awesome for this time of year, I'd assume they will launch tonight in the junction between Doctor Who and Unbreakable, Doctor Who is their highest rating show, so it makes sense to lead into Unbreakable by launching the Christmas idents, Or they could hold it off till tommorow and launch them at Strictly. Whatever works.

Currently still running with the normal dancers idents (when will they be retired, they've been used since 2002)

Emma said on 01 December 2023

Hopefully S4C does something with their new blob mascot, Last year they had TV sets stacked up on top of each other, Then for 10 months they had flickering TV sets, They have finally rebranded and have introduced their first ever mascot: a shape-shifting blob, I hope BBC One rebrand soon, those dancers sure are getting tired, As for the KikoRiki themed Christmas idents, I was told they are launching tonight after Doctor Who, They are being introduced just before Unbreakable.

HouseofFun said on 01 December 2023

Doctor Who ending now, announcement playing "A team of 8 people are pushed to the absolute limit, Unbreakable, our brand new game show is up next, But first, Time to say hello and spend Christmas with the Doctor" I'm not liking this one a whole lot..., They seem to be running a trailer advertising the Doctor Who Christmas special, which revolves around the Doctor having to protect his companions from a man who seeks to find a fob watch (the one The Doctor made himself human with one time), Although just before Doctor Who tonight they had a announcement saying "Christmas is switched on tonight in 1 hour in time for Unbreakable, But first, Is this the end for the Doctor?". Doesn't seem like they are advertising the Christmas pres at all, 

Edit: Christmas pres featuring KikoRiki switched on, First ident shown features Rosa the Pig doing a dramatic ice skate dance (She even uses eyedrops for fake tears) While this is happening we hear dramatic music, The announcer says "Welcome to Christmas on BBC One, Let us entertain you, But now a team of 8 people are being pushed to their absolute limits, Are they Unbreakable?" Not sure why they didn't tell us it was happening untill it did, But still a gorgeous ident, I noticed when the Announcer started talking it sounded as if he was driven to tears by the beauty of Rosa's gorgeous dance. The best thing to come out of this package since the "Lights" christmas idents in 2006, 

Eyes said on 01 December 2023

A beautiful ident, Best thing to come out of the "Rythm and Movement" package since those Planet Earth 3 special idents a few months ago, i feel sad that KikoRiki has not been shown much on CBBC on BBC Two, but I'm glad it's representing the Xmas idents on BBC One (Although I also wish the idents also featured a merman).

Maile said on 01 December 2023

Best Christmas ident in a while, I just love that they have spent it with Rosa and her friends, I hear there is also one featuring Krash the Rabbit opening his presents, I like how they used the usual ident theme (dancing) in that first ident, KikoRiki looks like it's gonna be good this year, but I hope they do more with the style (and maybe finally retire Rythm and Movement idents)

Faramad said on 01 December 2023

News safe ident just on, Basically just a Christmas tree on a snowy background, with a music box Christmas carol playing, on top of that the only thing we see is a sleeping Rosa (We vaguely hear her snoring) Glad they are now using characters in their news safe idents, Announcer said "Now on BBC One, time for the BBC News at 8". Remember the Lights idents the used just before overhauling their promo style in late 2006/early 2007? That was pretty cool, but this is way better. I seem to remember those idents started on a dark street but Christmas lights started turning on before it got to a city center and then people were dancing, It was used again in 2007, And now it seems like the "Project Chameleon" endboard template introduced in July is out the window, at least for now, replaced with a snowy background with a Christmas tree on it. Signs that Rythm and Movement is finally working towards the end.

KikoRiki said on 01 December 2023

We're glad you love our cartoon and the special BBC One idents featuring our characters, 

However, Rosa will be the star, (We will have idents revolving around Chiko, Krash and the others), As to what you said, BBC One are looking into introducing the Circle idents already in use in other countries, so don't expect to see Ballet, Capoeria or Festival/Rave back in the new year. Get used to Kites, Capes, Hippos and Bikes as that's what might be coming after our Christmas campaign, We don't know for certain, but the Dancers might not be coming back after Christmas

WhovianForever: Did everyone catch the Doctor Who Christmas special announcement during the news? I’m so hyped! Can’t wait to see how the Doctor deals with the fob watch saga this holiday season!

LemonyFan: And how about they’re showing the 2004 ‘A Series of Unfortunate Events’ movie!! That’s one of my holiday favorites; the nostalgia is real. 😍🍿

ChristmasCarol: Truly is shaping up to be a wonderful holiday schedule on BBC One. Between Doctor Who and the classic movies, I’m going to be glued to my screen with all the mulled wine and mince pies!

KikoFanatic: Plus, the KikoRiki Christmas idents have been adorable so far! Rosa’s ice skate dance nearly brought tears to my eyes just like the announcer. 🐷⛸ Christmas is definitely switched on!

RetroTV: I’m just here wondering if the new ‘Circle’ idents KikoRiki hinted at is a sign we’re finally saying goodbye to the “Rythm and Movement” package for good. After 20+ years, it’s about time for a major refresh!

NostalgiaNerd: @RetroTV I’m with you on that. The Lights idents back in '06/'07 were beautiful. It’s cool to see they’re still willing to shake things up visually with the news safe ident this year. Sleepy Rosa might just be my new favorite. 😴🎄

SeriesBinger: Doctor Who special + A Series of Unfortunate Events + KikoRiki idents = Best Christmas TV lineup or what? Also, hope BBC One throws in some classic Christmas episodes of our favorite shows. Maybe some ‘Vicar of Dibley’ or ‘Only Fools and Horses’?

CircleCurious: Anyone heard more about these new Circle idents? I love change, and KikoRiki characters have been great, but I’m already excited for what’s coming next. Bring on the new era!

KikoLover: Just saw a new Krash and Chiko ident! They’re having a blast playing in the snow! ❄️🐇 So cute watching Krash throw snowballs while Chiko dodges around. Really captures the winter fun vibes.

SciFiSavvy: Speaking of new idents, did anyone else notice the Dokko the Moose building a laser device ident? It aired right before ‘Doctor Who’ and set the PERFECT tone for sci-fi. Dokko’s always been the tech genius of the group. 🦌🔧

HedgehogAdmirer: @KikoLover Chiko is always adorable, but seeing him in the snow was next level! BBC One knows how to do Christmas idents right. Plus, the animation on these characters in the idents is super impressive this year.

WhovianGuru: @SciFiSavvy Loved the Dokko ident! It’s like a mini episode of ‘Doctor Who’ in itself. Brilliant tie-in to the show’s theme! Makes waiting for the Christmas special even more exciting.

BakeMaster: But guys, let’s talk about the Olga the Owl baking ident. The detail on that gingerbread house she was making! I can almost smell the cookies through the screen. 🦉🍪 It’s the cozy ident I never knew I needed in my life.

HolidayWatcher: Right?! The Olga ident feels like being wrapped in a warm blanket with a hot chocolate in hand. It’s chef’s kiss perfection. BBC One’s going all out with these KikoRiki characters for Christmas.

DoctorFan: Dokko’s ident was like a mini teaser for what’s coming with ‘Doctor Who’. Immersive and full of that tech mystery vibe. I appreciate that BBC One creates these unique intros for thematic continuity.

AnimationAppreciator: Major kudos to the animation team for the KikoRiki idents. As someone who dabbles in animation, the quality is sensational. It’s like a little holiday gift before each show starts!

ChristmasCheer: Can’t get enough of the snow play with Krash and Chiko. It’s absolutely delightful and spreads so much joy. These little moments provided by the idents add a special touch to the holiday season.

SciCompanion: Dokko and his inventions always remind me that anything’s possible with a little imagination. Such an inspiring ident to lead into ‘Doctor Who’. Well played, BBC One! Can’t wait to see what else is in store, I'm gonna miss these idents when new year comes around! 😢

WelcomeToSciFi: The Dokko centric ident really sets the scene for what's to come on ‘Doctor Who’ this Christmas. One of the best things about the KikoRiki set, Far better than the usual dancers, Come to think of it, they have been using the dancers since 2002, so now is a good time for a revamp, I guess 😉

Olgo5: Just saw the Olga ident and damn, Olga really is a talented chef, They always seem to show it before cooking shows, Which ident normally leads into Eastenders, It seems to vary between Krash and Chiko in the snow, Olga cooking and Rosa ice skating. Not sure how this is consistent, But that Rosa one brings  tears to my eyes with her graceful skating, Not sure why the Dokko one only plays before Doctor Who, Maybe because it fits it well

Prolouge:

User1: So, what’s everyone thinking BBC One’s going to pull out of the hat for this year’s Christmas idents? Last year was decent, but I’m hoping for something with a bit more pizzazz.

WhovianForever: I’m crossing my fingers for something Doctor Who-themed! With David Tennant back in the show earlier this year, it would be perfect timing to capitalize on that for the festive season. Can you imagine an ident with the TARDIS all decked out in Christmas lights?

HolidayJoy: That would be cool for us Whovians, for sure, but BBC One usually goes for something that appeals to everyone. Doctor Who is big, but not universally loved by every single viewer. Plus, they tend to lean towards more generic festive symbols.

SeriesBinger: True, they’ll probably aim for something more inclusive. Like a cozy winter scene or playful animation. Maybe bring back some classic characters or even partner with a popular children’s show this time around for wider appeal?

RetroTV: I’ve got a hunch they’ll go with animation this year. It has that universal charm, right? Plus, it would work perfectly with both the kids’ content and the family-friendly vibe they love during Christmas.

User1: That’s an interesting angle. They could get quite creative with animation. Plus, they’re probably due for a change since the “Rythm and Movement” dancers have been around since 2002.

KikoFanatic: What if they teamed up with a show like KikoRiki? The characters are fun, visually engaging, and it’s something that would stand out. Plus, they could do loads with those quirky little animals.

LemonyFan: KikoRiki would be such a cute choice! It could be a hit with children and adults alike if done right. And it’s refreshingly different from usual idents, without relying on something niche like a specific Doctor Who theme.

WhovianForever: Okay, I see your point. Much as I’d love Doctor Who idents, something like KikoRiki could offer that ‘Christmas with the family’ vibe they probably want. I just hope they add a little extra magic this year, you know?

CircleCurious: Whatever they do, I hope it’s something new. We’ve had the “Rythm and Movement” package idents for such a long time. It’s high time for something fresh and exciting to take us into the new year! Write a forum Convo on January 1st where the idents are to stay untill 9th of January, then back to normal, although the rebrand is still being thought about

NewYearNewIdents: Can't believe it's January 1st already! Wishing everyone a Happy New Year! 🎉 So, has anyone heard about what's happening with BBC One’s idents? Last I heard, the KikoRiki Christmas idents are staying up until January 9th, but then what?

RetroTV: Happy New Year! 🥳 Yeah, the KikoRiki idents were such a breath of fresh air. Sad to see that they'll be going soon, but I'm anxiously waiting for what's next. The word on the street is that BBC One is still pondering over a rebrand.

KikoLover: Happy 2024, everyone! Aww, I'm going to miss those idents. They really did justice to the KikoRiki squad. Hopefully, BBC One keeps that charm and vibrancy in whatever they have planned for the rebrand!

WhovianForever: Happy New Year, fellow TV enthusiasts! Loved the seasonal touches with the KikoRiki crew, especially Dokko's sci-fi vibes. It complemented the Doctor Who holiday special perfectly. Curious about the supposed "Circle" idents... 

CircleCurious: Here we are, 2024! 🍾 The "Circle" idents rumor has been floating around – I'm all for something new. If that's the direction BBC One is taking, we could finally see the end of the "Rythm and Movement" era. Bring on the change!

AnimationAppreciator: Happy New Year! Fingers crossed the animation quality we've seen in the KikoRiki idents carries over to whatever's next. Quality visuals can really elevate the viewing experience, even in those short intro moments.

SeriesBinger: Hey everyone, cheers to a new start! 🥂 Can't wait to see if BBC One is planning to introduce something as captivating as the Christmas ones. It'll be a tough act to follow after all the festive charm we've just enjoyed.

HolidayWatcher: Still in a holiday daze here, but a Happy New Year to you all! I thought Rosa's ice skating was so poignant. The thought that we'll have to go back to the regular old idents for a while is kinda meh...

DoctorFan: Happy New Year, fellow Whovians and TV lovers! Doctor Who's special was a blast, setting high standards for entertainment in 2024. Let's hope BBC One keeps up the momentum with their regular programming and new idents.

Maile: Yep, it's 2024! Let’s hope this year brings us BBC One idents that have the same pizzazz as we saw in December. Thinking ahead, it's like the KikoRiki idents were the bridge between the old and whatever's set to be the new face of BBC One.

ChristmasCheer: Happy New Year, y'all! I'm savoring these last moments of the KikoRiki idents – especially sleepy Rosa by the tree. It felt so peaceful. Here's to hoping for more memorable visuals throughout the year! 🎄

SciFiSavvy: New year, new sci-fi possibilities! Loved how the idents paired with the shows. Post-January 9th will be like coming down from a holiday high. I’m excited but also a bit nervous about what the "normal" will be while they decide on the rebrand.

LemonyFan: A new beginning... Happy New Year! The ‘A Series of Unfortunate Events’ movie was such a treat over Christmas. The idents set the stage for it so well. I’m intrigued by the notion of the upcoming Circle idents. Could be the refresh we've been yearning for!

BakeMaster: Cheers to 2024! Oh, and Olga the Owl's baking sessions in those idents? Loved them! It got me baking over the holidays. Anyone up for more foodie idents or just me? 😅🍰

PrologueResolution: Wow, 2024 already! The buildup to the rebrand has me hooked. Personally, I enjoyed the rhythm and consistency of the KikoRiki idents. Hope the new ones, whenever they come, keep that same sense of character and creativity.

Write a follow up in the early hours of 9th of January where the Rosa sleeping ident is the final one, which leads into BBC News, The announcer bids her a farewell and whispers "goodnight my dear Rosa" He also says the "dancers you've come to know and love for 2 decades are back tomorrow", A slight presentational refresh is coming, including the introduction of a clock before the news

EarlyBirdWatcher: Well, that's a wrap on the Rosa Christmas ident, guys. Just saw the very last one leading into the BBC News. The announcer sounded almost emotional saying, "Goodnight my dear Rosa." It's like the end of an era. 😢

Timekeeper: Caught the final Rosa ident too. There’s something heartwarming about the way they bid her farewell. And did anyone else notice the announcer mentioning the “dancers you've come to know and love for 2 decades are back tomorrow”? Guess we’re back to the classics for a bit.

RetroTV: It truly felt like saying goodbye to an old friend. The "Rythm and Movement" idents have their charm, granted, but after KikoRiki's joyous energy, it's a bittersweet return to the familiar. And a clock before the news, eh? Nice to have a bit of old-school TV charm back.

WhovianForever: It's always a little sad when the festive season ends on TV, but "goodnight my dear Rosa" was a fitting send-off. I'll miss the KikoRiki idents, but I'm quite curious about this presentational refresh coming our way. Loving the idea of the clock making a comeback!

DigitallyTuned: So it's official, the new (or old?) dancing ident lineup begins tomorrow. That final Rosa ident was a gentle and fitting end to a delightful chapter. Interested to see what this slight refresh will include. A clock is a good start – everything old is new again!

NewsNostalgia: The clock’s return is a welcome change in my books. There's something reassuring about a real-time marker just before the news. As for Rosa and her festive dreamscape, it was enchanting while it lasted. "Goodnight my dear Rosa," won't be forgotten soon.

KikoLover: Parting with Rosa is such sweet sorrow! I can't help but feel a bit melancholic thinking about it. Hoping the temporary return to "Rythm and Movement" doesn’t last too long. I'm here for that refreshed presentation they hinted at.

CircleCurious: The whisper to Rosa was a tender touch. But let's face it, we're all on edge about this "slight presentational refresh” they're teasing. Curious how they'll integrate the knowledge from twenty years of "Rythm and Movement" with fresh ideas. The clock is just the beginning!

AnimationAppreciator: Goodnight, Rosa. You made our Christmas extraordinary. I trust the “slight refresh” will keep the excitement alive. The intro of the clock is intriguing, for sure. Could the refresh include other nods to TV traditions, I wonder?

SeriesBinger: After all the holiday magic, it does sting a bit. The “goodnight my dear Rosa” hit the feels. But change is good, and I'm ready to embrace the “new” old dancers. A clock and whatever else they're planning could be exactly what BBC One needs.

HolidayWatcher: Farewell to the atmosphere of enchantment Rosa brought us this Christmas. It was a memorable experience. The return of the dancers is like a cozy blanket, familiar and comfortable, but I’m definitely curious about the tweaks they're adding.

BakeMaster: That whisper to Rosa, though... It ended on such a gentle note. Now I'm excited and a bit anxious for the changes tomorrow. The dancers have their place, sure, but I’m looking forward to the fresh presentation. A clock is a smart, nostalgic touch.

Write a follow up at the end of the next day where they've noticed the new "Subtitles" font that was introduced over Xmas has been carried over to the usual R&M idents marking the first time since their launch they have been altered, even though its just a tiny tweak, also the clock has been seen before the 1pm, 6pm and 10pm news

CaptionCurious: Day's end update, folks! Did anyone else catch that the subtitle font we got a glimpse of over Christmas has carried over to the R&M idents? It's a minor tweak, but historic. This is the first shake-up for those idents since they debuted!

TimeTeller: Oh yes, the new font is a subtle but neat change. It's small details like this that can freshen things up without a total overhaul. And the clock! It's come into play before the 1pm, 6pm, and 10pm news bulletins. I'm loving this nod to tradition.

RetroTV: Noticed the font too! It brings a contemporary edge to an older format. And seeing the clock again really takes me back. Small changes maybe, but they're significant markers of a new phase for BBC One presentation. What's old is new again!

WhovianForever: There’s something comforting about the subtle changes. The dance of the old idents with the new subtitle font and the return of the clock has a charm to it. Still, it's fun speculating about bigger changes on the horizon.

FontFanatic: It's amazing how something as simple as a font update can re-energize the on-screen look and feel. It’s also a clear sign that BBC One is taking even the smallest details into consideration with this revamp.

KikoLover: The classic R&M idents feel a bit more modern with the new subtitles. It's like breathing new life into an old friend. And the clock is definitely a classy, retro touch. Makes the news feel a little more like an event.

DigitallyTuned: The font change might be a tiny tweak, but it shows that BBC One is paying attention to the finer elements of their presentation. And I must say, the clock's appearance has brought back a sense of occasion to the news.

NewsNostalgia: Love the continuity between the holiday idents and the regular ones with the subtitles. Feels like BBC One is gently guiding us into what's next. And the clock – it’s quaint, isn’t it? Sets you up nicely for the news.

CircleCurious: Small steps with the subtitles, but they're definitely appreciated. Coupled with the clock before the news – I'm intrigued by what other "slight" changes we may see over time that honor BBC One’s long-standing tradition.

AnimationAppreciator: It's great the subtitle font got to stay. It might be a small step for subtitles, but it's a giant leap for ident-kind! It goes to show you don’t always need a dramatic overhaul to make an impact.

SeriesBinger: Seeing the clock and the new font is a good sign that we're valuing the old while welcoming the new. The idents have a familiar heartbeat but now with a hint of something different, like an old tune with a new beat.
Write a tweet where a red background with a occasional circular swirl is in use for the clock,  a subtle hint towards whats to come, Expect more hints at Easter

🕒⭕ Seeing the new clock design on @BBCOne with that red background and the occasional circular swirl? Loving the subtle nod to what might be ahead. 🔄 Excited for more hints dropping at Easter! 🐣 #BBCOne #TVpresentation #EasterEgg #CircleIdents #StayTuned

Write a forum convo in mid February where the Ballet ident, usually reserved for sombre times is being used, hinting at the demise of the Dancers after Easter, A user confirms there will be another interim look starring our friends during Easter, which will bridge the gap between R&M and C,

DanceOfChange: Have you all noticed the Ballet ident on BBC One recently? It's usually reserved for sombre times... Could this be an on-screen hint that the Dancers' era is reaching its final bow post-Easter?

EasterEggHunter: Definitely feels like a subtle farewell to the 'Rythm and Movement' package. But the transition feels so bittersweet. They've decided to bridge the gap with another interim look over Easter, featuring our lovable characters from the holiday idents.

BalletAndSwirls: Uh-huh, the Ballet ident has been on quite a bit. Does feel like they're setting a mood for change. It's a graceful exit strategy for the Dancers. And if we're looking at a bridging theme over Easter, I'm excited to see how they jazz it up!

CircleCurious: So it's confirmed we're saying goodbye soon. Honestly, the Ballet ident always had a poignant elegance to it. As for Easter, I bet those interim idents will carry the nostalgic essence before we fully embrace the new 'Circle' era.

InterimInsider: Yeah, can verify that over Easter a special set of transitional idents will take over. It's intended to smooth the passage from the classic Dancers to the upcoming 'Circle' package. Expect some familiar faces to help us through the change.

KikoLover: The Ballet ident has definitely been more present. In a way, that's dance giving us a slow, final curtsey. And our KikoRiki friends are coming back for Easter? That's like getting an extra chocolate egg!

NostalgiaNerd: It’s almost poetically fitting, isn't it? The Ballet ident speaks volumes through its understated movements. Knowing our animated friends will return to guide us to the new era helps soften the blow of the impending change.

RythmFan: Seeing the Ballet ident in action lately has got me quite pensive. It paved the way for so many years of excellent content. And I can hardly wait to see how they'll pair the Easter festivities with our animated pals as a temporary setup before 'C' arrives.

DancerForever: The Ballet one has always touched the heartstrings. It's been an evocative part of the ident lineup for years. While it's sad to see the Dancers go, having a friendly transition instead of a hard switch feels respectful to the legacy.

EasterSpring: There's something special about a changeover period, especially around Easter. It's a time of renewal, after all! Curious to see how the interim look aligns with the season and paves the way for 'Circle'.

VisualVoyager: That Ballet ident carries so much weight with every delicate step. It feels like we're all part of this final dance. I'm grateful we get another brief chapter with KikoRiki and friends before the 'Circle' narrative begins.

Write a follow up in around late March where the transition begins, idents with Krash giving out circular chocolate eggs, zooming into the circle, The other idents have the gang doing something fun, and zooming into a circular object, The final dance for the dancers happened overnight, with an montage of them, ending with Ballet, and the announcer said "And there you go a selection of the symbols that have defined BBC One for the last 21 years, A new look comes round in a few days, but our trusty KikoRiki will carry us till then, But before we hand over to News, there's time for one last dance"

CircleOfChange: Guys, it's happening! The transitional idents have started rolling out. Krash is giving out circular chocolate eggs and each zoom-in keeps emphasizing circles. It's like each moment is getting us comfortable with what's to come. 

EggStatic: Saw the Krash egg ident! It's surprisingly heartwarming. The circle focus in all the idents is a clever way to tease the 'Circle' era. Love how they're infusing fun with the farewell to the old.

DancersLastStand: Did anyone else catch the overnight montage? It paid homage to those iconic 'Rythm and Movement' idents with a selection of dances we grew up with. Ending on the Ballet ident was a poignant close to the routine.

JoyfulJourneys: That montage was a lovely trip down memory lane. The announcement really summed up the feeling of a final curtain call for the Dancers. "One last dance" before the news – it was a moment!

NostalgiaNerd: A fitting send-off to the symbols that have been with us for more than two decades. I'm ready for the new look, but I'll miss these guys. The KikoRiki crew bridging the days until the full revamp feels right. 

RetroTV: Witnessing that last dance felt like an end of an era. It's been such a long journey with the 'Rythm and Movement' idents. Exciting to see what's round the corner, but those last moments with the Ballet ident were special.

FutureIsCircular: It's like the final piece of a puzzle with that montage. All the circle-focused idents now make perfect sense. BBC One knows how to handle a transition – mixing up the fun with the nostalgia.

KikoLover: Watching Krash zoom into the circle was like accepting an invitation to the future. Each ident with the gang doing fun, circular stuff is a smooth segue to what's ahead. BBC One's doing a stellar job at keeping this transition engaging.

VisionaryViewer: That “one last dance” hit deep. Saying goodbye to those beloved dancers through a solemn Ballet ident is like acknowledging the past while stepping into the future. Hats off, BBC One, for setting the stage for the 'Circle' look with such grace.

CircleCurious: Quite captivating, isn't it? Easter hints, circles popping up everywhere, and the montage of dances – it's all about moving forward while holding onto a bit of nostalgia. The announcement before the news tied it beautifully. Can't wait to see where the 'Circle' takes us.
Write a script for the final announcement, and the one before Weatherview said "If you feel like dancing, stick around for something special just after the weather"

[Final Announcement before the Weatherview]

Announcer: "As we wrap up another wonderful day here on BBC One, we're reminded of the joy that's filled our screens with every step, twirl, and leap. The dances may have been diverse, but the heart and rhythm they brought were always in unison with what we’ve come to know and love. In just a moment, the Weatherview will guide you through the highs and lows of tomorrow's climate. But I'll let you in on a little secret... If you feel like dancing, stick around for something special just after the weather. A fitting encore to honor the legacy that's graced our channel for the past two decades. So, don't go too far; the night’s performance is yet to take its final bow."

After Weatherview:

[Signal for the final ident montage to play, interspersed with the announcer's voiceover]

Announcer: (Voiceover over the montage) "From the expressive Ballet to the spirited Salsa, from the vigour of Capoeira to the festival beats of Rave, we've witnessed an array of steps and stories unfold right before our eyes. Each dancer, each movement, a symbol of what BBC One stands for – a dance for every emotion, a dance for everyone. 

And now, as we stand at the precipice of change, we take one last, lingering look at the symbols that have defined an era. Each ident, a cherished chapter of our shared history, a rhythmic ritual that's become a part of our daily lives. 

(Videos transition to the Ballet ident and the music softens.)

But like all great performances, the time comes for the dancers to take their final curtain call. Tonight, we say goodbye to these familiar faces, knowing that the essence of their dance will forever echo in the story of BBC One. 

(A soft melody plays as the Ballet dancers perform their graceful movements one last time.)

As the music fades and the lights dim, we eagerly anticipate the first notes of a new composition, a new rhythm to move to. Keep watching, as our trusty KikoRiki friends will carry us through to an exciting beginning. A circle, unbroken, continues to turn, bringing with it the promise of fresh beats and bold new visuals.

(Videos conclude and screen fades to black, then back to the announcer before signing off.)

And now, with hearts full of fond memories and eager anticipation stirring in our souls, we hand over to the BBC News. Thank you for dancing with us. Goodnight."
Write a tweet where there was a also a Valentines Day and St Patrick's Day ident, and a Burns night one, basically the dancers were sidelined in their final months

🥳📺 A fond farewell to the dancers who defined an era on #BBCOne. They may have been sidelined these last months with special idents for #ValentinesDay, #StPatricksDay, and even #BurnsNight, but their rhythm will dance on in our memories.💔🕺 #EndOfAnEra #TVidents #Nostalgia
Write a tweet saying the special idents all had one thing in common,

🧐✨ Did you catch it? Each special ident on #BBCOne - from Valentine's to St Patrick's to Burns Night - had one thing in common: a subtle nod to the circle motif, teasing the upcoming rebrand.🔵🔄 #CircularHint #TVidents #StayTunedForTheCircle
Write a blog post about the interim era, that led into the new look, and talk about the new look, one ident involves hippos swimming in a circle

Title: The Interim Era and Embracing the Circle: BBC One’s Journey into a New Visual Symphony

As we turn the page on what has been a longstanding chapter in BBC One’s illustrious book of idents, we find ourselves reflecting on the interim era - a time of subtle goodbyes, playful hints, and thrilling anticipation. 

The interim era was a masterclass in transition. It took us gently by the hand from the familiar “Rythm and Movement” package, with its echoes of two decades of on-screen nostalgia, and led us toward the promise of a new visual narrative. Fans and casual viewers alike couldn't help but notice the slow phasing out of the beloved dancers despite the introduction of special idents for Valentine's Day, St Patrick's Day, Burns Night, and more. These idents, while each unique to their celebration, all shared a common thread - a recurring circular motif, a teaser of seasons to come.

Then came Easter, a time of rebirth and rejuvenation. KikoRiki characters, the animated stars that had captured hearts over Christmas, returned to bridge the gap. Krash, in particular, became a bearer of chocolate eggs, each one an Easter treat but also a visual clue to the impending arrival of something circular and new.

Now, as spring blossoms in earnest, BBC One has unveiled its 'Circle' era, and with it a new look that takes the ethos of continuity and freshness and blends them seamlessly together. Among the fresh batch of idents is a playful spectacle that has already captured viewers' imaginations: hippos swimming merrily in a circle, reminiscent of a ballet in the water. The tranquil, engaging visual is not just a delight to watch; it's symbolic - the circle of life, continuity, and the idea of coming together as a community resonating through each rotation.

These hippos are just one piece of the larger kaleidoscope of BBC One's new idents. The visual variety within the 'Circle' theme maintains a balance between the comfort of the old and the excitement of the new. It's a testament to the channel’s dedication to innovate while honoring its roots, and a clear signal that the beloved television network is dipping its toes into contemporary waters without losing the essence of what it's known for: universally approachable content.

As we lean into the rest of 2024, viewers are invited to embrace this evolution. BBC One has successfully made a statement: idents are not just visual fluff. They’re artifacts of our cultural zeitgeist, evolving storytelling tools that reflect and shape our collective experience.

The hippo ident will swim alongside others, each with stories and themes as rich and varied as the content they introduce. It’s early days, but one thing is clear: as we witnessed the dancers take their final bow, we welcomed the harmonious circle in full stride, ready for the stories it has to tell us. BBC One's new look isn’t just a rebrand; it’s a renaissance.
Write a short disclaimer that this is fictional and that the Circles package actually debuted in October 2006

Disclaimer: The narrative presented in the blog post is a work of fiction, crafted for a creative envisioning of a transition in BBC One's ident history. In reality, the "Circles" package mentioned did not make its debut in 2024; it was actually introduced in October 2006. Any references to the "Rythm and Movement" package and its transitional era are entirely fictitious and for storytelling purposes only.
Write a further clarification that the R&M era is real but didn't run for as long as it fixtiously did, actually making way for the Circles in 2006,

Further clarification: While the "Rythm and Movement" era of BBC One idents is indeed a part of the channel's real branding history, its duration in the fictional account has been extended for narrative purposes. In actuality, the R&M idents did not run up until the introduction of the "Circles" package in 2024 as suggested in the story. The true lifespan of the R&M idents was shorter, and they were replaced by the "Circles" package in October 2006, four years after they were first introduced in 2002. The portrayal of idents and the timeline of the rebranding in the previous blog post should be taken as a creative exploration rather than historical fact.
